title: Thermo follow-up idea
content: Need a personal-safe follow-up workflow that can draft next steps after a strategy meeting.

title: Need a quick decision on Friday agenda
content: Can you confirm whether we should move the strategy block to Friday afternoon and who needs to review the deck before then? If I do not hear back by tomorrow noon I will lock the current agenda.

title: Need a quick decision on Friday agenda
content: Can you confirm whether we should move the strategy block to Friday afternoon and who needs to review the deck before then? If I do not hear back by tomorrow noon I will lock the current agenda.

title: Morning capture
content: Remember to keep Catalyst personal and connector-honest.

title: Find AI-driven family savings and side-hustle opportunities we can execute over the next 30 days
content: Use AI to find and execute a small set of high-probability family savings and side-hustle plays in the next 30 days, with Catalyst as the tracking layer. The win is not “big transformation.” It’s a few practical moves that lower friction, recover cash, and test revenue without creating chaos.

title: Create a family portfolio hypothesis around book writing, consulting, and social media leverage
content: Build Catalyst into a family “idea-to-asset” engine where books, consulting, and social media all feed one another instead of living as separate efforts. The core hypothesis is that one strong point of view, packaged well, can create both income and durable family leverage.

title: use AI to audit subscriptions and lower our monthly expenses
content: Use AI to surface every recurring charge, sort them by value and frequency, and build a clean cancellation/downgrade shortlist before the next billing cycle.

title: build a simple family savings tracker for recurring subscriptions
content: Build a very simple recurring subscription tracker that shows what the family is paying each month, who uses it, and what could be saved if it were canceled. The likely win is not just savings; it is visibility. Most subscription waste survives because nobody has a clean list.
